name: com.lge.gcuv
label: Gcuv
description: GCUV Not 100% sure but @siraltus from XDA thinks it refers to "Gauce Components" which seems to be the LG's version of CSC (carrier sales code - automatic carrier-specific customization). It gets run on first boot after factory reset, sets up the ROM features based on which carrier and country code is specified in the build.prop, and then gets frozen so it doesn't reconfigure things on subsequent boots. It's basically the only person to mention "Gauce components" on the web (other than restricted LG webpages when using Google dorks). https://forum.xda-developers.com/tmobile-lg-v10/development/rom-lg-v10-h901-10c-debranded-debloated-t3277305/page12/page12

label: Mlt
description: LG MLT Run in background all the time and probably serves purpose to help LG remote support. The thing is this acts as a good spyware. name: com.lge.theme.blackgold
label: Blackgold
description: LG Black Bold theme. Safe to remove, but also probably pointless to do so as most theme packages are just data containers. Make sure you don't delete the package for the theme you're currently using. I don't know what will happen then.

name: com.lge.theme.highcontrast
label: Highcontrast
description: LG High Contrast theme. Safe to remove, but also probably pointless to do so as most theme packages are just data containers. Make sure you don't delete the package for the theme you're currently using, I don't know what will happen then.

name: com.lge.theme.titan
label: Titan
description: LG Titan theme (labelled 'Platinum' in the theming app). Safe to remove, but also probably pointless to do so as most theme packages are just data containers. Make sure you don't delete the package for the theme you're currently using. I don't know what will happen then.

name: com.lge.theme.white
label: White
description: LG White theme. Safe to remove, but also probably pointless to do so as most theme packages are just data containers. Make sure you don't delete the package for the theme you're currently using, I don't know what will happen then.

name: com.lge.themeinstaller
label: Themeinstaller
description: Needed to install themes.

name: com.lge.themeservice
label: Themeservice
description: Needed to apply themes.

name: com.lge.themesquare
label: Themesquare
description: Change the theme on LG UX.

name: com.lge.touchcontrol
label: Touchcontrol
description: I have never seen this menu in the settings app. I say it's safe to remove. I can't think of any use case for this setting, it just allows you to change where you're allowed to touch the screen

name: com.lge.updatecenter
label: Updatecenter
description: LG Update Center Provide Android upgrade and LG updates (Settings --> System --> Update Center) I believe you won't receive any updates if this packages is deleted.

name: com.lge.upsell
label: Upsell
description: Contains dialogs from different carriers about upgrading the mobile plan Should be safe to remove?

name: com.lge.video.vr.wallpaper
label: Wallpaper
description: Video Wallpaper LG 360° VR Wallpapers

name: com.lge.videoplayer
label: Videoplayer
description: LG Video Player NB : This is a bad one. VLC is much better.

name: com.lge.videostudio
label: Videostudio
description: Quick Video Editor Allows you to create and edit video files using the videos (and photos) stored on the phone. https://www.lg.com/us/mobile-phones/VS980/Userguide/281.html

name: com.lge.voicecare
label: Voicecare
description: LG Voice care Allows you to use your device if the touch screen or display is damaged. You must agree to location-based information use and personal information collection to use Voice Care. https://www.lg.com/hk_en/support/product-help/CT20136018-20150122834174-others

name: com.lge.voicerecorder
label: Voicerecorder
description: LG Voice Recorder Simple voice recording app. Safe to remove.

name: com.lge.vrplayer
label: Vrplayer
description: LG VR player Enables you to watch 360° pictures/videos.

name: com.lge.wallpaperpicker
label: Wallpaperpicker
description: Wallpaper Needed for wallpaper preview.

name: com.lge.wapservice
label: Wapservice
description: Icon looks like email configuration. I'd say it's safe to remove. Probably related to the stock email app.

name: com.lge.wernicke
label: Wernicke
description: QVoice Engine Needed by Q-voice (the LG Q Voice voice assistant) to work.

name: com.lge.wernicke.nlp
label: Nlp
description: Natural-language processing for LG intelligent assistant. Used to understand what a human is saying when they speak. Needed by QVoice

name: com.lge.wfd.spmirroring.source
label: Source
description: Provide wifi-direct feature Note : Wifi-direct is Wi-Fi standard enabling devices to easily connect with each other without requiring a wireless access point. It allows allows two devices to establish a direct Wi-Fi connection without requiring a wireless router https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wi-Fi_Direct spmirroring = ??? screen p... mirroring ?

name: com.lge.wfds.service.v3
label: V3
description: Wifi-direct service (v3) See above.

name: com.lge.wifi.p2p
label: P2P
description: LG P2p Service Wifi-drect P2P allows the device to discover the services of nearby devices directly, without being connected to a network. Needed for LG Wifi-direct feature. https://developer.android.com/training/connect-devices-wirelessly/nsd-wifi-direct

name: com.lge.wificall
label: Wificall
description: Needed for Wi-Fi Calling on LG devices.

name: com.lge.wifisettings
label: Wifisettings
description: WiFi settings It can be found in settings.
